co.crystaldev.alpinecore.util

Class CollectionUtils

  • java.lang.Object
    • co.crystaldev.alpinecore.util.CollectionUtils

  • public final class CollectionUtils
extends java.lang.Object
    Utility for instantiating commonly used Collections with concise and clean syntax.
    Since:
    0.1.1

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      CollectionUtils() 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static <T> @NotNull java.util.LinkedList<T> linkedList(T... items)
      Creates a new ordered list with the specified objects.
      static <K,V> @NotNull java.util.LinkedHashMap<K,V> linkedMap(K firstKey, V firstValue, java.lang.Object... items)
      Creates a new ordered map with the specified objects.
      static <T> @NotNull java.util.LinkedHashSet<T> linkedSet(T... items)
      Creates a new ordered list with the specified objects.
      static <T> @NotNull java.util.List<T> list(T... items)
      Creates a new list with the specified objects.
      static <K,V> @NotNull java.util.Map<K,V> map(K firstKey, V firstValue, java.lang.Object... items)
      Creates a new map with the specified objects.
      static <T> @NotNull java.util.Set<T> set(T... items)

    • Method Detail

      • list

        @SafeVarargs
 @NotNull
 @Contract(value="null -> fail")
public static <T> @NotNull java.util.List<T> list(@NotNull
                                                                                                             T... items)
        Creates a new list with the specified objects.

        This method makes no assertion as to the ordering of the list. For an ordered list, use linkedList(Object[]).

        Type Parameters:
        T - the type of the elements in the list
        Parameters:
        items - the objects to add to the list
        Returns:
        the list
        See Also:
        ArrayList

      • linkedList

        @SafeVarargs
 @NotNull
 @Contract(value="null -> fail")
public static <T> @NotNull java.util.LinkedList<T> linkedList(@NotNull
                                                                                                                         T... items)
        Creates a new ordered list with the specified objects.
        Type Parameters:
        T - the type of the elements in the list
        Parameters:
        items - the objects to add to the list
        Returns:
        the list
        See Also:
        LinkedList

      • set

        @SafeVarargs
 @NotNull
 @Contract(value="null -> fail")
public static <T> @NotNull java.util.Set<T> set(@NotNull
                                                                                                           T... items)
        Creates a new set with the specified objects.

        This method makes no assertion as to the ordering of the list. For an ordered list, use linkedSet(Object[]).

        Type Parameters:
        T - the type of the elements in the set
        Parameters:
        items - the objects to add to the set
        Returns:
        the set
        See Also:
        HashSet

      • linkedSet

        @SafeVarargs
 @NotNull
 @Contract(value="null -> fail")
public static <T> @NotNull java.util.LinkedHashSet<T> linkedSet(@NotNull
                                                                                                                           T... items)
        Creates a new ordered list with the specified objects.
        Type Parameters:
        T - the type of the elements in the set
        Parameters:
        items - the objects to add to the set
        Returns:
        the set
        See Also:
        LinkedHashSet

      • map

        @NotNull
public static <K,V> @NotNull java.util.Map<K,V> map(@NotNull
                                                              K firstKey,
                                                              @NotNull
                                                              V firstValue,
                                                              @NotNull
                                                              java.lang.Object... items)
        Creates a new map with the specified objects.

        This method makes no assertion as to the ordering of the map. For an ordered map, use linkedMap(Object, Object, Object...).

        Varargs should be specified by alternating keys and values.

        If the number of varargs is odd, the method will quietly ignore them.

        Type Parameters:
        K - the type of the keys in the map
        V - the type of the values in the map
        Parameters:
        items - the keys and values to add to the map
        Returns:
        the map
        See Also:
        HashMap

      • linkedMap

        @NotNull
public static <K,V> @NotNull java.util.LinkedHashMap<K,V> linkedMap(@NotNull
                                                                              K firstKey,
                                                                              @NotNull
                                                                              V firstValue,
                                                                              @NotNull
                                                                              java.lang.Object... items)
        Creates a new ordered map with the specified objects.

        Varargs should be specified by alternating keys and values.

        If the number of varargs is odd, the method will quietly ignore them.

        Type Parameters:
        K - the type of the keys in the map
        V - the type of the values in the map
        Parameters:
        items - the keys and values to add to the map
        Returns:
        the map
        See Also:
        LinkedHashMap
